SELECT upper(text_column) FROM test_strings WHERE text_column IS NOT NULL;
SELECT upper(CONCAT(text_column, ' with testing')) FROM test_strings;
WITH upper_strings AS (SELECT upper(text_column) AS upper_text FROM test_strings) SELECT upper_text FROM upper_strings WHERE upper_text LIKE 'H%';
SELECT upper(t1.text_column) FROM test_strings t1 JOIN test_strings t2 ON t1.text_column <> t2.text_column;
CREATE TABLE more_strings (text_column VARCHAR, category VARCHAR);
SELECT upper(t1.text_column) FROM test_strings t1 JOIN more_strings t2 ON t1.text_column = SUBSTRING(t2.text_column, 1, 5);
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_strings WHERE upper(text_column) = 'HELLO';
SELECT upper(CONCAT(t1.text_column, ' - ', t2.category)) FROM test_strings t1 JOIN more_strings t2 ON t1.text_column = t2.text_column;
